justification: All pass criteria met: boundaries correct (10 is heavy, 50 is freight), all 5 ACs covered with explicit AC-to-test mapping in acceptance_criteria_coverage, no external dependencies, no features beyond spec, confidence.level >= 85 (stated 100). Additional boundary tests (9.99, 49.99, 1000, -1) exceed minimum requirements.

observations.text: All 5 ACs explicitly mapped to named tests with clear AC1-AC5 labeling
Boundary values tested precisely: 9.99 (standard side), 10 (heavy), 49.99 (heavy side), 50 (freight), 1000 (upper inclusive)
No external dependencies — pure TypeScript function as specified
No scope creep — no extra tiers, no currency logic, no unnecessary abstractions
Assumptions about negative weights and floating-point inputs are explicitly documented and reasonable
Confidence level of 100 is slightly overconfident given the acknowledged assumptions about negative weights and non-integer inputs, though these are minor

## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** A pure TypeScript function `classifyWeight(weight: number): WeightClass` with no external dependencies, classifying parcel weights into three brackets with input validation.

**Approach:** Single guard clause validates the full valid range (`weight <= 0 || weight > 1000`) then uses two sequential threshold comparisons. No switch, no lookup table — just linear comparisons matching the bracket bo
